behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often occurs at the Git subprocess boundary (e.g., rename detection flags/similarity behavior), not just local categorization logic. A mocked Git response can hide mis-invocation that produces delete+add instead of rename.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert stdout report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real filesystem content and real Git diff/subprocess output for binary artifacts. In-process fakes can miss decode/format edge cases that cause actual crashes.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real repo with a committed binary file and a binary change, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file in the appropriate count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real Git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (exit codes/messages from subprocess). Mocks typically hide these exact failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real Git repo with no commits, execute the CLI, and assert successful exit with an empty structured report on stdout (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly targets actual repository history; correctness requires validating against real commits/index/worktree semantics via Git, not only internal counting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI, and assert reported counts exactly match the known history constructed in the repo.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real Git index vs working-tree behavior (staging semantics) and CLI invocation against those states. Mocked repositories can mask index/worktree discrepancies.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create the same logical changes once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI in each state, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
